## Radiant Cherry Butterfly Game Implementation

### Game Description

Radiant Cherry Butterfly is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing their pieces (represented as butterflies) on the board. The goal is to create a radiant cherry pattern, which is a line of five pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Game Rules

#### Equipment

The game is played on a 5x5 square board.

#### Start Rules

The game starts with an empty board.

#### Play Rules

Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. A player can only place a piece on an empty square.

#### End Rules

The game ends when a player creates a radiant cherry pattern or when the board is full.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Radiant Cherry Butterfly"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Additional Rules

Let's add some additional rules to make the game more interesting.

#### Capturing Pieces

A player can capture an opponent's piece by placing their own piece on an adjacent square.

```ludax
(game "Radiant Cherry Butterfly"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

#### Radiant Cherry Pattern

A player wins if they create a radiant cherry pattern of five pieces in a row.

```ludax
(game "Radiant Cherry Butterfly"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Complete Implementation

Here is the complete implementation of the Radiant Cherry Butterfly game:

```ludax
(game "Radiant Cherry Butterfly"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            ; No start rules
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (or (line 5) (line 5 orientation:vertical) (line 5 orientation:diagonal)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Industrious Micro Bumblebee Game Implementation

The Industrious Micro Bumblebee game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a set of rules that involve placing and moving pieces.

### Game Rules

The game starts with an empty board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The goal is to create a connected line of pieces that spans the entire board.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Industrious Micro Bumblebee"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            ; No starting pieces
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

* The game is played on a 5x5 rectangular board.
* The game starts with no pieces on the board.
* Players take turns placing their pieces on empty spaces on the board.
* The game ends when a player creates a connected line of 5 pieces, or when the board is full.

### Adding More Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add more rules. For example, we can introduce a "nectar" mechanism, where players can collect nectar by creating connected lines of pieces.

```lark
(game "Industrious Micro Bumblebee"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
        (nectar 0)
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            ; No starting pieces
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                        (if (line 3) (increment_score mover 2))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

* We added a "nectar" equipment with an initial value of 0.
* When a player places a piece, they increment their score by 1.
* If a player creates a connected line of 3 pieces, they increment their score by 2.
* The game ends when a player's score reaches 10, or when the board is full.

### Further Enhancements

We can further enhance the game by introducing more complex rules, such as:

* Allowing players to move pieces instead of just placing them.
* Introducing obstacles or blocked spaces on the board.
* Creating a "hive" mechanism, where players can create a connected group of pieces that cannot be broken.

These enhancements would require more complex Ludax code and a deeper understanding of the game's mechanics.

## Ludicrous Duck Of Pride Game Implementation

The Ludicrous Duck Of Pride is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a size of 8x8. The objective of the game is to create a line of 4 ducks in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Game Rules

1. The game starts with an empty board.
2. Player 1 (P1) places a duck on a random empty square.
3. Player 2 (P2) places a duck on a random empty square.
4. Players alternate turns.
5. On each turn, a player can place a duck on an empty square.
6. A player wins if they create a line of 4 ducks in a row.
7. If the board is full and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Ludicrous Duck Of Pride"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (random empty))
            (place P2 (random empty))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding More Rules

Let's add more rules to make the game more interesting. We'll introduce a "pride" mechanism, where a player can create a "pride" of 3 ducks in a row. If a player creates a pride, their opponent must place their next duck adjacent to the pride.

### Updated Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Ludicrous Duck Of Pride"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
        (variable ?pride (exists (line 3)))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (random empty))
            (place P2 (random empty))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (if ?pride 
                            (adjacent (line 3))
                            empty
                        )
                    )
                )
                (if ?pride 
                    (set ?pride false)
                )
                (set ?pride (exists (line 3)))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding More Complexity

Let's add more complexity to the game by introducing different types of ducks. We'll have two types of ducks: "normal" ducks and "proud" ducks. Proud ducks can be used to create a pride, while normal ducks cannot.

### Updated Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Ludicrous Duck Of Pride"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
        (variable ?pride (exists (line 3 proud)))
        (variable ?normal_ducks (count (occupied normal)))
        (variable ?proud_ducks (count (occupied proud)))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (random empty) (type normal))
            (place P2 (random empty) (type normal))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination 
                        (if ?pride 
                            (adjacent (line 3 proud))
                            empty
                        )
                    )
                    (type 
                        (if (>= ?normal_ducks ?proud_ducks) 
                            normal 
                            proud
                        )
                    )
                )
                (set ?normal_ducks (count (occupied normal)))
                (set ?proud_ducks (count (occupied proud)))
                (if ?pride 
                    (set ?pride false)
                )
                (set ?pride (exists (line 3 proud)))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Devious Fanatic Caracara

Devious Fanatic Caracara is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board with a grid of cells. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board, trying to create lines of their pieces while blocking their opponent.

### Game Definition

```ludax
(game "Devious Fanatic Caracara"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (10))
            (place P2 (40))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Piece Movement

In this version, pieces can move to adjacent empty cells.

```ludax
(game "Devious Fanatic Caracara"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (10))
            (place P2 (40))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (adjacent empty))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Capture

In this version, a piece can capture an opponent's piece by jumping over it to an empty cell.

```ludax
(game "Devious Fanatic Caracara"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (10))
            (place P2 (40))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (adjacent empty))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and (adjacent occupied) (adjacent empty)))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring

In this version, players score points for each line of pieces they create.

```ludax
(game "Devious Fanatic Caracara"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (10))
            (place P2 (40))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (adjacent empty))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and (adjacent occupied) (adjacent empty)))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Final Version

The final version of the game includes all the features mentioned above.

```ludax
(game "Devious Fanatic Caracara"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (10))
            (place P2 (40))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (adjacent empty))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and (adjacent occupied) (adjacent empty)))
                    (mover_reference MOVER)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation

### Game Description

Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing their pieces on empty squares. The game ends when one player has five pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Game Implementation in Ludax

```ludax
(game "Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

In this version, we add a start rule that places the first piece of player P1 in the center of the board.

```ludax
(game "Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Play Effects

In this version, we add a play effect that captures adjacent pieces of the opponent.

```ludax
(game "Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding End Rules

In this version, we add an end rule that checks if the mover has won by having five pieces in a row.

```ludax
(game "Orthodox Cicada Of Recreation"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied opponent))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (line 4) (mover_is P1)) (P1 win))
            (if (and (line 4) (mover_is P2)) (P2 win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Great Roaring Chachalaca

### Game Description

Great Roaring Chachalaca is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a rectangular board with a unique mechanic where players can move and capture pieces in a specific pattern.

### Simplified Rules

The simplified rules are as follows:

* The game is played on a 5x5 rectangular board.
* Player 1 (P1) starts by placing a piece on the center square.
* Players alternate turns.
* A player can move a piece to an adjacent empty square.
* A player can capture an opponent's piece by jumping over it to an empty square.
* The game ends when one player has no pieces left or when the board is full.

### Ludax Implementation

```ludax
(game "Great Roaring Chachalaca"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 2))
        )
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination (and empty center)))
            )
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent occupied)))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (not (exists occupied)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Incremental Rules

To make the game more interesting, we can add the following rules:

* A player can only capture an opponent's piece if it is in a specific pattern (e.g., a diagonal).
* A player can move a piece to a square that is two spaces away if there is an opponent's piece in between.

### Ludax Implementation (Incremental Rules)

```ludax
(game "Great Roaring Chachalaca"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 5 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 2))
        )
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination (and empty center)))
            )
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (adjacent occupied direction:diagonal)))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied direction:diagonal) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (custodial 2 occupied)))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2 occupied) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (not (exists occupied)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Further Enhancements

We can further enhance the game by adding more rules, such as:

* Implementing a scoring system.
* Introducing special pieces with unique abilities.
* Adding a timer to limit the number of moves.

These enhancements can be implemented in Ludax by adding more rules, effects, and predicates.

## Delightful Magnificent Terrier Game Implementation

The Delightful Magnificent Terrier game is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 grid, where players take turns placing their terrier pieces. The goal is to create a row of five terriers in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).

### Game Rules

1. The game starts with an empty 5x5 grid.
2. Player 1 (P1) places their first terrier on the grid.
3. Player 2 (P2) places their first terrier on the grid.
4. Players alternate turns, with P1 going first.
5. On each turn, a player can place one terrier on an empty space on the grid.
6. The game ends when a player creates a row of five terriers in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally).
7. If the grid is completely filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Delightful Magnificent Terrier"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (center))
            (place P2 (adjacent (center) direction: right))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Explanation

* The game is defined with two players and a 5x5 grid equipment.
* The start rules place the first two terriers on the grid, one for each player, at the center and adjacent to the center.
* The play rules allow players to take turns placing terriers on empty spaces on the grid.
* The end rules check if a player has created a row of five terriers in any direction. If so, that player wins. If the grid is completely filled and no player has won, the game is a draw.

### Incremental Additions

To make the game more interesting, we can add additional rules, such as:

* Allowing players to capture opponent terriers by surrounding them.
* Introducing special "power" terriers that have unique abilities.

These additions can be implemented by modifying the play and end rules, and adding new effects and predicates to the game.

### Example Use Case

To play the game, simply run the Ludax interpreter with the game definition file. The game will start with an empty grid, and players can take turns placing terriers on the grid. The game will end when a player wins or the grid is completely filled.

Note: This is a basic implementation of the Delightful Magnificent Terrier game. You can modify and extend the game rules to make it more complex and interesting.

## Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph

Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. The first player to create a line of five pieces in any direction (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) wins the game.

### Simplified Rules

```ludax
(game "Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph"

    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))

        )
    )
)
```

```ludax
(game "Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph"
```

### Adding End Rules

Let's add an end rule to check if the game is won by a player.

```ludax
(game "Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (3 3))

            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )

            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (count (occupied mover)) 10) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))

            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

```ludax
(game "Giga Lorikeet Of Triumph"

    (rules

            (place P1 (3 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent occupied))
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (count (occupied mover)) 10) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (count (occupied mover)) 10) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

## Solemn Woodoo Tench

### Game Description

Solemn Woodoo Tench is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a 5x5 square board. Players take turns placing stones on empty intersections. A player wins if they have five stones in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

### Ludax Implementation

```lark
(game "Solemn Woodoo Tench"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Start Rules

In this version, we add a start rule that places a stone for each player at the center of the board.

```lark
(game "Solemn Woodoo Tench"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Win Conditions

In this version, we add win conditions for both players.

```lark
(game "Solemn Woodoo Tench"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (line 5) (mover_is P2)) (P1 lose))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Rendering Details

In this version, we add rendering details for both players.

```lark
(game "Solemn Woodoo Tench"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (2 2))
            (place P2 (2 3))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (and (line 5) (mover_is P2)) (P1 lose))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)
```

## Cautious Flat Parakeet Game Implementation

### Game Description

Cautious Flat Parakeet is a two-player abstract strategy game. The game is played on a square board of size 5x5. Players take turns placing their pieces on the board. A player can only place a piece on an empty square that is not adjacent to any of their opponent's pieces.

### Initial Implementation

Here is the initial implementation of the game in Ludax:

```ludax
(game "Cautious Flat Parakeet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent occupied))))
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Scoring and Winning Conditions

Let's add scoring and winning conditions to the game. A player wins if they have 5 pieces in a row, either horizontally, vertically, or diagonally.

```ludax
(game "Cautious Flat Parakeet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent occupied))))
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

### Adding Forced Pass

Let's add a forced pass rule. A player must pass if they have no valid moves.

```ludax
(game "Cautious Flat Parakeet"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 5))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (start
            (place P1 (13))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent occupied))))
                    (effects 
                        (increment_score mover 1)
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)
```

This implementation provides a basic structure for the Cautious Flat Parakeet game. You can further modify and extend the rules to make the game more complex and interesting.

prompt:
Invent simple rules for a novel two player abstract strategy game called Conscious Truthful Koel. Implement it in the ludax language. You will find attached the ludax's grammar as well as a few examples of games implemented in ludax. Start by implementing a simplified version of your rules, and then incrementally add rules that are harder to express in ludax. At each step, make sure you write a compilable game according to ludax's grammar.

        grammar.lark:
// ---Root---
game: "(" "game" name players equipment rules rendering? ")"

// ---Players---
players: "(" "players" positive_int ")"

// ---Equipment---
equipment: "(" "equipment" board ")"
board: "(" "board" (board_square | board_rectangle | board_hexagon | board_hex_rectangle) ")"
board_square: "(" "square" number ")"
board_rectangle: "(" "rectangle" number number ")"
board_hexagon: "(" "hexagon"  number ")"
board_hex_rectangle: "(" "hex_rectangle" number number ")"

// ---Rules---
rules: "(" "rules" start_rules? play_rules end_rules ")"

// ---Start rules---
start_rules: "(" "start" start_rule+ ")"
start_rule: start_place
start_place: "(" "place" player_reference pattern_arg ")"

// ---Play rules---
play_rules: "(" "play" play_phase+ ")"
play_phase: phase_once_through | phase_repeat
phase_once_through: "(" "once_through" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
phase_repeat: "(" "repeat" play_mover_order play_mechanic ")"
play_mover_order: "(" player_reference+ ")"

play_mechanic: play_place force_pass? 
play_place: "(" "place" mover_reference? place_destination_constraint place_result_constraint? play_effects?")"
force_pass: "(" "force_pass" ")"

// ---Constraints---
place_destination_constraint: "(" "destination" super_mask ")"
place_result_constraint: "(" "result" super_predicate ")"

// ---Effects---
play_effects: "(" "effects" play_effect+ ")"
play_effect: effect_capture 
           | effect_flip
           | effect_increment_score
           | effect_set_score

effect_capture: "(" "capture" super_mask mover_reference? increment_score_arg? ")"
effect_flip: "(" "flip" super_mask mover_reference? ")"
effect_increment_score: "(" "increment_score" mover_reference function ")"
effect_set_score: "(" "set_score" mover_reference function ")"

// ---Functions---
function: function_add
        | function_connected
        | function_constant
        | function_count
        | function_line
        | function_multiply
        | function_score
        | function_subtract

function_add: "(" "add" function+ ")"
function_connected: "(" "connected" multi_mask_arg mover_reference? direction_arg? ")"
function_constant: positive_int
function_count: "(" "count" super_mask ")"
function_line: "(" "line" positive_int orientation_arg? exact_arg? ")"
function_multiply: "(" "multiply" function+ ")"
function_score: "(" "score" mover_reference ")"
function_subtract: "(" "subtract" function function ")"

// ---End rules---
end_rules: "(" "end" end_rule+ ")"
end_rule: "(" "if" super_predicate end_rule_result ")"
?end_rule_result: result_win | result_lose | result_draw | result_by_score

// -- Result definitions --
result_win: "(" mover_reference "win" ")"
result_lose: "(" mover_reference "lose" ")"
result_draw: "(" "draw" ")"
result_by_score: "(" "by_score" ")"

// -- Mask definitions --
super_mask: mask | super_mask_and | super_mask_or | super_mask_not
super_mask_and: "(" "and" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_or: "(" "or" super_mask+ ")"
super_mask_not: "(" "not" super_mask ")"

mask: mask_adjacent
    | mask_center
    | mask_corners
    | mask_custodial
    | mask_edge
    | mask_empty
    | mask_occupied
    | mask_pattern
    | mask_prev_move

mask_adjacent: "(" "adjacent" super_mask direction_arg? ")"
mask_center: "center"
mask_corners: "corners"
mask_custodial: "(" "custodial" custodial_length_arg mover_reference? orientation_arg? ")"
mask_edge: "(" "edge" edge ")"
mask_empty: "empty"
mask_occupied: "occupied" | "(" "occupied" mover_reference ")"
mask_pattern: "(" "pattern" dimensions_arg pattern_arg rotate_arg? ")"
mask_prev_move: "(" "prev_move" mover_reference ")"

// "Multi-masks" are special keywords that are manually split into multiple
// sub-masks at compile time. This is mostly useful for the "connected" function,
// which expects a list of masks to check for connections between
multi_mask: multi_mask_corners
          | multi_mask_edges
          | multi_mask_edges_no_corners

multi_mask_corners: "corners"
multi_mask_edges: "edges"
multi_mask_edges_no_corners: "edgesNoCorners"

// ---Predicate definitions---
super_predicate: predicate | super_predicate_and | super_predicate_or | super_predicate_not
super_predicate_and: "(" "and" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_or: "(" "or" super_predicate+ ")"
super_predicate_not: "(" "not" super_predicate ")"

predicate: predicate_equals
         | predicate_exists
         | predicate_full_board
         | predicate_function
         | predicate_greater_equals
         | predicate_less_equals
         | predicate_mover_is
         | predicate_passed

predicate_equals: "(" "=" function+ ")"
predicate_exists: "(" "exists" super_mask ")" // technically equivalent to (>= (count mask) 1) 
predicate_full_board: "(" "full_board" ")"
predicate_function: function // special syntax which is equivalent to "(>= function 1)"
predicate_greater_equals: "(" ">=" function function ")"
predicate_less_equals: "(" "<=" function function ")"
predicate_mover_is: "(" "mover_is" player_reference ")"
predicate_passed: "(" "passed" (mover_reference | BOTH) ")"

// Additional (potentially optional) arguments for predicates
custodial_length_arg: ANY | positive_int
dimensions_arg: "(" positive_int positive_int ")"
direction_arg: "direction:" direction
exact_arg: "exact:" boolean
increment_score_arg: "increment_score:" boolean
multi_mask_arg: multi_mask | "(" super_mask+ ")"
orientation_arg: "orientation:" orientation
pattern_arg: "(" positive_int+ ")"
rotate_arg: "rotate:" boolean

// Optional rendering details
rendering: "(" "rendering" rendering_detail+ ")"
rendering_detail: color_assignment

color_assignment: "(" "color" player_reference color ")"

// General-purpose definitions
?number: SIGNED_NUMBER
?positive_int: /[0-9]+/
?boolean: TRUE | FALSE
?edge: TOP | BOTTOM | LEFT | RIGHT | TOP_LEFT | TOP_RIGHT | BOTTOM_LEFT | BOTTOM_RIGHT
?direction: UP | DOWN | LEFT | RIGHT | UP_LEFT | UP_RIGHT | DOWN_LEFT | DOWN_RIGHT | VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?orientation: VERTICAL | HORIZONTAL | ORTHOGONAL | DIAGONAL | BACK_DIAGONAL | FORWARD_DIAGONAL | ANY
?color: WHITE | BLACK
// ----------------------------

?player_reference: P1 | P2
?mover_reference: MOVER | OPPONENT
name: STRING
variable_name: /\?[a-z][a-z0-9]*/
id: /[a-zA-Z0-9_]+/

// Constants
TOP: "top"
BOTTOM: "bottom"
UP: "up"
DOWN: "down"
LEFT: "left"
RIGHT: "right"
TOP_LEFT: "top_left"
TOP_RIGHT: "top_right"
BOTTOM_LEFT: "bottom_left"
BOTTOM_RIGHT: "bottom_right"
UP_LEFT: "up_left"
UP_RIGHT: "up_right"
DOWN_LEFT: "down_left"
DOWN_RIGHT: "down_right"
VERTICAL: "vertical"
HORIZONTAL: "horizontal"
ORTHOGONAL: "orthogonal"
DIAGONAL: "diagonal"
BACK_DIAGONAL: "back_diagonal"
FORWARD_DIAGONAL: "forward_diagonal"
ANY: "any"
TRUE: "true"
FALSE: "false"
MOVER: "mover"
OPPONENT: "opponent"
P1: "P1"
P2: "P2"
BOTH: "both"
WHITE: "white"
BLACK: "black"

// Lark imports
%import common.ESCAPED_STRING -> STRING
%import common.SIGNED_NUMBER
%import common.WS
%ignore WS


        example games:
        

(game "Complexity Demo" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 10))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination (and empty (not (adjacent (adjacent (prev_move opponent))))))
                    (effects 
                        (capture (adjacent (prev_move mover)) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 5) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Four"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (rectangle 6 7))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination (and empty (or (edge bottom) (adjacent occupied direction:up)))))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Connect-Six"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 6) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Hex"
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hex_rectangle 11 11))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge top) (edge bottom))) 2) (mover_is P1)) (mover win))
            (if (and (>= (connected ((edge left) (edge right))) 2) (mover_is P2)) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Gomoku" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 15))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 5 exact:true) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Pente" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment
        (board (square 19))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination center))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P1)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (effects 
                        (capture (custodial 2) increment_score:true)
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 5) (mover win))
            (if (>= (score mover) 10) (mover win))
        )
    )
)


(game "Reversi" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 8))
    ) 
    
    (rules
        (start
            (place P1 (28 35))
            (place P2 (27 36))
        )
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place 
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (exists
                            (custodial any)
                        )
                    )
                    (effects 
                        (flip (custodial any))
                        (set_score mover (count (occupied mover)))
                        (set_score opponent (count (occupied opponent)))
                    )
                )
                (force_pass)
            )
        )
        
        (end
            (if (passed both) (by_score))    
        )
    )
    (rendering
        (color P1 black)
        (color P2 white)
    )
)


(game "Tic-Tac-Toe" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 3))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        
        (end 
            (if (line 3) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))    
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalath" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (hexagon 9))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play 
            (repeat (P1 P2)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (line 4) (mover win))
            (if (line 3) (mover lose))
        )
    )
)


(game "Yavalax" 
    (players 2)
    (equipment 
        (board (square 13))
    ) 
    
    (rules 
        (play
            (once_through (P1)
                (place (destination empty))
            )
            (repeat (P2 P2 P1 P1)
                (place
                    (destination empty)
                    (result 
                        (and 
                            (not (line 5))
                            (not (= (line 4) 1))
                        )
                    )
                )
            )
        )
        (end 
            (if (>= (line 4) 2) (mover win))
            (if (full_board) (draw))
        )
    )
)
